key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 parent EPS ¥76.81 normalized down for clearing pass-through / volume cyclicality
Peak FY2025 activity; base owner earnings below spot EPS 76.81

open_questions: Confirm JPY price; separate core trading fees from clearing pass-through in normalized earnings.
Hold override?** Croupier sleeve incumbent vs **3.2%** total synthesis IRR at spot.
Re-run extract on **`20251113_j.pdf`** (encoding failure) and **`E_ER_JPX_Q2FY2025.pdf`** for filing-verified H1 FY2026 flow.
Entry math: what ADV/revenue decline restores ~15% IRR at ¥2,000?
thesis: Japan Exchange Group (JPX) operates Japan's dominant stock exchange, clearing house, and market-data platform. **[Fact]** Brokers, issuers, and clearing members pay trading, clearing, listing, and data fees. Revenue scales with cash-equity turnover and clearing collateral balances, not proprietary trading on core operations. **[Fact]** FY2025 (Mar-2026) operating revenue was **¥198.7B** (+22.5%) with operating margin ~58.5% and ROE 23.1% (scan-tier Q4 FY2025 release; see Primary sources). Cash equities average daily turnover reached **¥7.52T** (+31.9% YoY). The group holds a regulatory and network monopoly on domestic market infrastructure.
why_market_wrong: No clear mispricing signal. **[Inference]** FY2025 was exceptional: retail and inbound equity engagement drove ADV +31.9% and clearing collateral income surged. **[Inference]** FY2027 management forecast implies flat-to-down operating income vs the FY2025 peak, so the market may correctly price cycle-peak earnings at ~26× P/E. Return is franchise earnings at price unless volume normalizes and the multiple compresses further.
